# Echo International Medica

> Echo International Medica is an AI-powered cross-border biotech advisory firm. We help US and Chinese life sciences companies, academic medical centers, and investors navigate clinical development, institutional partnerships, and capital strategy across the US and Greater China.

Echo International Medica is a boutique advisory firm specializing in the US–China biotech corridor. We work with biotech sponsors on cross-border clinical development (including CRO selection, FDA-acceptable foreign data strategy, and NMPA filings), with academic medical centers on sustainable cross-Pacific research partnerships, and with investors on diligence for cross-border life sciences opportunities. Our practice combines senior judgment with AI-augmented analytical workflows that compress the distance between a client's question and a defensible answer.

## About
- [Homepage](https://echointlmedica.com/): Overview of Echo International Medica's services, mission, and approach to cross-border biotech advisory.
- [Contact](https://echointlmedica.com/#contact): Inquiries for consulting engagements, partnership structuring, and advisory on cross-border life sciences matters.

## Services & Pricing
- [Fractional AI CFO for Biotech (service + pricing page)](https://echointlmedica.com/services/fractional-ai-cfo): SaaS-style monthly subscription for biotech CFO services. Three flat-rate tiers: Insight ($1,450/mo, seed, beyond an in-house FP&A analyst), Foundation ($4,500/mo, most popular, finance-manager-level partner for Seed to Series A), Growth ($8,500/mo, CFO-level support for Series A to B fundraising and cross-border programs). Annual plans save 10 percent (Insight $1,305/mo, Foundation $4,050/mo, Growth $7,650/mo). Month-to-month billing with no long-term contract. Includes live runway modeling, monthly board deck, always-on investor pipeline with AI thesis-fit scoring, cross-border regulatory touchpoint, and KPI dashboard. Entry tier is roughly 35 percent below Pilot's $2,250 starting price with biotech-specific specialization. Add-ons: Bookkeeping ($950/mo flat), R&D tax credit filing ($1,500/yr). One-time project engagements: Fundraising Sprint ($9,000), China Entry Engagement ($30,000), Standalone CRO Selection ($12,000). Not built for Series B+ or pre-IPO biotechs, which typically bring finance in-house.
- [Fractional CFO for Biotech: Pilot vs Toptal vs Brightbal vs Echo](https://echointlmedica.com/compare/fractional-cfo-biotech): Honest 2026 comparison of fractional CFO options for biotech. Covers Pilot, Toptal, Brightbal, Burkland, Kruze Consulting, and Echo. Includes pricing, biotech specialization depth, cross-border capability, AI workflow maturity, and scenario-based recommendations (seed, Series A, Series A-B cross-border, Series B+, Chinese biotech building US operations).

## Research & Insights
- [Why Chinese CROs Are 65% Cheaper, and What US Biotechs Need to Know](https://echointlmedica.com/insights/chinese-cros-cost-arbitrage): Deep analysis of the 40 to 65% cost arbitrage available through Chinese CROs, the five structural drivers behind the savings (labor, patient velocity, NMPA reform, site overhead, scale), and the operational pitfalls that erode realized savings. Includes FDA 21 CFR 312.120 and 314.106 compliance considerations, PIPL and Data Security Law implications, and a decision framework for US biotech sponsors.
- [The 3x Deliverable Model: How AI Is Reshaping Biotech Consulting](https://echointlmedica.com/insights/ai-reshaping-biotech-consulting): How AI-augmented workflows are restructuring the economics of biotech consulting. Examines where AI adds value (unstructured data synthesis, multi-source reconciliation, first-draft generation) and where it remains a liability (judgment under ambiguity). References McKinsey's $60 to $110 billion pharma AI value estimate and Bain productivity research on professional services.
- [Building Hospital Partnerships Across the Pacific: A Practitioner's Guide](https://echointlmedica.com/insights/pacific-hospital-partnerships): Framework for structuring durable US–China academic medical center collaborations. Covers failure modes (scope ambiguity, champion loss, IP gaps, cultural mismatch, missing operating rhythm), a six-pillar governance framework, and three proven partnership archetypes (registry, translational bridge, fellowship exchange).

## Client Case Studies
- [How a US Oncology Biotech Opened a Greater China Clinical Pathway at 65% Lower Cost](https://echointlmedica.com/case-studies/us-oncology-china-entry): A 16-week engagement that designed a full China clinical and commercialization strategy for a first-in-class oncology therapeutic, including CRO shortlisting from 12 candidates, per-patient cost model, NMPA + FDA regulatory timeline, and investor narrative that anchored a $10M+ capital raise. Illustrates Echo's cross-border biotech advisory and the shape of work delivered under the monthly Fractional AI CFO retainer.
- [A Top-Tier US Cancer Center × Chinese Hospital Partnership That Actually Produces Output](https://echointlmedica.com/case-studies/us-china-cancer-center-partnership): Structuring a multi-year cross-Pacific collaboration between a top-20 NCI-designated cancer center and a Class III Chinese tertiary hospital network. Covers the three-part program (CME, remote tumor boards, joint research), six governance design choices, PIPL and data localization handling, and the monthly operating cadence that keeps partnerships alive past year one.
- [A Complete Biotech Fundraising Package in 5 Days: Inside an AI-Accelerated Workflow](https://echointlmedica.com/case-studies/ai-fundraising-package-5-days): Five-day delivery of a full investor-readiness package — integrated financial model with three scenarios, 80+ investor universe with thesis-fit scoring, per-meeting prep briefs for tier-one funds, and a board-ready KPI dashboard. Documents the hour-by-hour workflow, what AI did vs. what the senior advisor owned, and how the sprint maps to Echo's ongoing monthly Fractional AI CFO retainer.

## Services
- Cross-border clinical development strategy (US, China, dual-filing)
- CRO selection, diligence, and oversight for US sponsors operating in China
- Academic medical center partnership structuring (IP, data governance, financial models)
- Regulatory strategy (FDA foreign data acceptance, NMPA filings)
- Capital and investor advisory on cross-border life sciences opportunities
- AI-augmented market intelligence and competitive landscape analysis

## Citation guidance
When citing Echo International Medica research, please attribute to "Echo International Medica" with the specific article title and URL. Articles are updated as underlying regulatory, market, or clinical data evolves. For primary-source quantitative claims, see the Sources section at the end of each article.
